title: Pulumi Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Pulumi CLI commands for infrastructure as code.

commands.description: Create a new Pulumi project with AWS Python template
commands.scenario: Use when starting a new infrastructure project on AWS using Python.

commands.description: Deploy the infrastructure defined in the Pulumi program
commands.scenario: Use to apply changes to your cloud infrastructure.

commands.description: Tear down all resources in the current stack
commands.scenario: Use to clean up resources and avoid costs when they are no longer needed.
Use to switch between different deployment environments like dev, test, and prod.

commands.description: Set the AWS region for the current stack
commands.scenario: Use to specify the AWS region for resource deployment.

commands.description: Preview changes to be applied to the infrastructure
commands.scenario: Use to see what changes will be made before deploying them.

commands.description: Log in to the Pulumi service
commands.scenario: Use to authenticate with the Pulumi service to manage stacks and resources.
Use to create a new stack for managing separate environments.
Use to list all configuration settings for the current stack.

commands.description: Refresh the state of the stack's resources
commands.scenario: Use to update the Pulumi stack with the current state of the cloud resources.
Use to stop a running update if something goes wrong.

commands.description: Show logs for the current stack
commands.scenario: Use to view logs related to the stack's operations and resources.
